notes.label: Note 01
notes.title: On the particular light of late afternoon
notes.body: There is a light, around four o'clock in the colder months, that makes ordinary streets feel borrowed from memory. It doesn't ask for anything. It arrives, lays itself across a wall, and leaves again before you've decided what to do with it.
notes.aside: I have learned to stop for it — to let the errand wait.

notes.label: Note 02
notes.title: What the table remembers
notes.body:
A cup, a folded napkin, the last of the morning's bread. Small still lifes assemble themselves without my asking. For a while I tried to tidy them away. Now I leave them, because they are honest about how a day is actually lived.

None of it is composed. That is the part I am most fond of.

notes.label: Note 03
notes.title: A practice of looking twice
notes.body: Most things will give you a second meaning if you let them sit. The cracked step. The neighbour's washing line. The way steam leaves a window. I keep these pages as a way of looking twice — once with the eye, once on purpose.
notes.aside: You are welcome to look alongside me.
